name: A Season of War
text: Rally. (After this card is revealed in a province, add the top card of your dynasty deck to the province, faceup.) Action: Discard each card in each province, refilling each province faceup. End the dynasty phase. Then there is an additional dynasty phase, during which players do not collect fate.

name: Ancient Master
text: You may play this character as an attachment with the text: "Reaction: After attached character is declared as an attacker or defender – search the top 5 cards of your conflict deck for a Kihō or Tattoo card, reveal it, and add it to your hand. Shuffle.

name: Assassination
text: Conflict Action: Lose 3 honor. Choose a character with printed cost 2 or lower – discard that character. (Max 1 per round.)

versions.flavor: Steel whispered through silk and flesh. In the morning, they would find the body and speak of shadows and dishonor — but tonight, the deed was done.

name: Banzai!
text: Action: During a conflict, choose a participating character – that character gets +2[conflict-military] until the end of the conflict. You may lose 1 honor to resolve this ability twice. (Max 1 per conflict.)

name: Centipede Tattoo
text: Monk character only. Attached character gains Tattooed. Attached character does not bow as a result of conflict resolution in conflicts in which it lost.

versions.flavor: Inked with the blood of Togashi-kami, the centipede provides speed.

name: City of the Rich Frog
text: Eminent. (This province starts the game faceup and cannot be turned facedown. It cannot be a stronghold province.) After setup, fill this province to 3 cards. When you would refill this province, refill it to 3 cards instead of 1.

name: Court Games
text: [conflict-political] Conflict Action: Select one - either choose and honor a participating character you control, or your opponent chooses and dishonors a participating character they control. (Max 1 per conflict.)

versions.flavor: “We play today, so we can meet and play tomorrow.”

name: Cycle of Rebirth
text: Action: Choose a card in a province - shuffle this card and that card into their owner's decks. Refill each province emptied by this effect faceup. (Max 1 per round.)

versions.flavor: "This lifetime shall prepare you for the trial to come." - Togashi Hassuno

name: Defend Your Honor
text: Keeper role only. Interrupt: When the effects of an opponent's event would initiate during a conflict, initate a [conflict-military] duel against a character your opponent controls of his or her choice - resolve the duel. If your character is the duel's winner, cancel those effects.

name: Favorable Ground
text: Action: During a conflict, sacrifice this holding. Choose a character you control – move that character to the conflict or home from the conflict.

name: Finger of Jade
text: Attach to a character you control. Interrupt: When the effects of a card ability that targets attached character would initiate, sacrifice this attachment – cancel those effects.

versions.flavor: “The tears of Amaterasu, crystallized into protection against the darkness that seeps from Jigoku.”

name: Hawk Tattoo
text: Attach to a character you control. Attached character gains Tattooed. Reaction: After you play this attachment during a conflict – move attached character to the conflict. If attached character is a Monk, you may take another action after this one. Errata from RRG v1.6

name: High House of Light
text: Action: During a conflict, bow this stronghold. Choose a participating Monk character you control - that character cannot be chosen as the target of an opponent's event during this conflict. If you have played 5 or more cards in this conflict, also move 1 fate from a ring to this character.

name: Hurricane Punch
text: Action: During a conflict, choose a participating Monk character – that character gets +2[conflict-military] until the end of the conflict. Draw 1 card.

versions.flavor: "Strike with one thousand fists and you need deliver only a single blow."

name: Imperial Storehouse
text: Action: Sacrifice this holding – draw 1 card.

name: In Service to My Lord
text: This card can be played from your discard pile. Action: Bow a (friendly) non-unique character and choose a unique character - ready the chosen character. Put this card on the bottom of its owner's conflict deck.

name: Iron Foundations Stance
text: Action: During a conflict, choose a participating Monk character you control – until the end of the conflict, that character cannot be bowed or moved home by opponents' card effects. If you have played another Kihō in this conflict, draw 1 card.

name: Keeper Initiate
text: Keeper role only. Reaction: After you claim a ring that matches the element of your role – put this character into play from your dynasty discard pile or provinces. Then, put 1 fate on this character.

name: Keeper of Void
text: Increase your deckbuilding influence value by 3. Reaction: After you win a [element-void] conflict as the defending player – gain 1 fate.

name: Kitsuki Investigator
text: Action: While this character is participating in a [conflict-political] conflict, spend 1 fate to an unclaimed ring – look at your opponent's hand. Choose 1 of those cards and discard it. (Max 1 per conflict.)

name: Let Go
text: Action: Choose an attachment – discard it.

versions.flavor: The material world is but a distraction.
The Dragon's strike was not meant to kill, but to teach — and in that single moment of contact, his enemy's sword learned the lesson of impermanence.

name: Miya Mystic
text: Action: During the conflict phase, sacrifice this character. Choose an attachment – discard that attachment.

versions.flavor: "Most of my family speak to the citizens of the Empire. I speak to the kami of the land itself."
“The Miya motto speaks truth — there are no Miya heroes, only servants of balance. Today, I serve by returning chaos to order.”

name: Restoration of Balance
text: Interrupt: When this province is broken – your opponent discards cards from his or her hand until that player has 4 or fewer cards in hand. Errata from RRG v9

name: Revered Bonshō
text: When fate would be removed from characters during the fate phase, instead distribute that fate among unclaimed rings as evenly as possible.

versions.flavor: "The gong of the temple bell echoes the destiny that awaits us all."

name: Sacred Sanctuary
text: Reaction: After an attack is declared at this province, choose a Monk character you control – ready that character. Until the end of the conflict, that character cannot be bowed by opponents' card effects or as a result of conflict resolution.

name: Shintao Monastery
text: You are considered to have played an additional card in each conflict.

versions.flavor: With simple ritual and meditation, one lets go of the Three Sins: Desire, Fear and Regret.

name: Swell of Seafoam
text: Support. (Another player may help pay this card's fate cost.) Action: During a conflict, choose a participating Monk character – that character does not bow as the result of this conflict's resolution. If you have played another Kihō in this conflict, also honor that character.

name: Tattooed Wanderer
text: You may play this character as an attachment with the text: "Attached character gains covert."

versions.flavor: It is said that Dragon shugenja and courtiers are warriors, their warriors are monks, and their monks are inexplicable.

name: Teacher of Empty Thought
text: Action: During a conflict in which this character is participating, if you have played 3 or more cards during this conflict - draw 1 card.

versions.flavor: "You cannot hir me because you think you can hit me. I move with the flow of the kata, not with my mind, and am freed from the prison of thought."

name: The Pursuit of Justice
text: Action: During a conflict at a [element-water] province, choose a participating character - ready that character.

name: Togashi Acolyte
text: You may play this character as an attachment with the text: "Reaction: After you play a card during a conflict in which attached character is participating - attached character gets +1[conflict-military] and +1[conflict-political] until the end of the conflict. (Unlimited.)"

name: Togashi Ichi
text: Disguised Monk. Action: During a conflict in which this character is attacking, if 10 or more cards have been played during this conflict and if the attacked province is not a stronghold province - break that province.

name: Togashi Initiate
text: Action: While this character is attacking, spend 1 fate to an unclaimed ring – honor this character.

name: Togashi Mitsu
text: This character cannot be evaded by the covert keyword. Action: During a conflict in which this character is participating, if you have played 5 or more cards in the conflict, choose a ring - resolve that ring's effects as if you were the attacking player.

name: Togashi Tadakatsu
text: The defending player chooses the element (but not the type) during each conflict declared against him or her. This choice is made before conflict type, attacking characters, and the attacked province are chosen.

versions.flavor: The ant and the beetle are equal beneath the sun. The beetle and the ant are equal beneath the moon.

name: Tranquil Philosopher
text: Action: Choose an unclaimed ring - move 1 fate from that ring to another unclaimed ring. Then, gain 1 honor.

versions.flavor: "Enlightenment is the unbent blade of grass amidst a raging storm."

name: Upholding Authority
text: This province gets +2 strength while you have an Earth role. Interrupt: When this province is broken – the attacking player reveals his or her hand. Choose a card in their hand and discard any number of copies of that card from their hand.

name: Void Fist
text: Action: During a conflict, if you have played at least 2 other cards this conflict, choose a participating character with equal or lower [conflict-military] skill than a participating Monk character you control – move the chosen character home and bow it.

name: Way of the Dragon
text:
Attach to a character you control. Limit 1 per character.
You may trigger each of attached character's triggered abilities an additional time each round (or specified period).
